free_text: Mass distribution in 19-F induced fission of 232-Th.
Radiochemistry and Nuclear Physics
divisions.
BARC-TIFR 14UD Pelletron accelerator
Self-supporting thorium metal foils of thickness in the
range of 1.8 to 2.2 mg/cm2 were used in four
irradiations.
A superpure aluminum catcher foil of thickness 6.75
mg/cm2 was used to collect the fission products in the
forward direction.
Beam current was in the range of 15 to 20 Na
"particle" for 112 MeV and 30 to 40 Na for 95 MeV
irradiations.
At each energy, a short irradiation for 30 min
and a long irradiation for 5 H were performed to
measure the yields of short lived and long lived
fission products, respectively.
An electron suppressed Faraday cup
The activities of the short irradiation samples
were followed for three days and those of the long
irradiation samples were followed for about three
months.
An accuracy of the absolute gamma-ray detection
efficiency of the detector
Total error
The formation cross section of a fission product was
obtained by applying a correction for the fission
products emitted in the backward direction. The
correction factors were evaluated by the measurement of
fission product activities in the catcher foil and the
target separately and assuming a forward-to-backward
(F/B) ratio of 1.1. The correction factors were
evaluated for the typical fission products 92-Sr,
105-Rh, 122-Sb, 130-I, 139-Ba, and 142-La. The factors
were constant within the experimental errors and an
average value of 1.81+/-0.08 was used.
Last checking has been done.
